title: 250 years of American independence: A disenchanted anniversary
subhead: 'America 250' (13/13). Trump's two terms in office are undermining national cohesion. The ideal of equality enshrined in the founding documents is a distant prospect, the system of checks and balances has cracked and any reform seems impossible. Americans' belief in their shared history is eroding. Even the nature of the system of government is a matter of debate. But historians are trying to put things into perspective.

title: Devastating heatwave leaves France's wildlife in crisis

US gripped by extreme heatwave, disrupting 250th anniversary and World Cup celebrations
subhead: Temperatures neared 40°C (104°F) in New York, Philadelphia and Washington DC, on Thursday and Friday, causing widespread power outages and prompting organizers to delay the start of Independence Day celebrations.

title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants in visit to Lampedusa island
subhead: Pope Leo XIV visited the island, a key destination for those risking the perilous Mediterranean crossing from Africa to Europe, and delivered a speech calling on the US and EU to change their migration strategies.

title: New Zealand open Nations Championship with slim victory over France
subhead: Cam Roigard and Will Jordan scored two tries each as the All Blacks edged out France 34-32 in a nine-try thriller that kicked off the first Nations Championship.

title: The incredible success of the de Gaulle biopic 'reflects the paradox embodied by the hero of Free France'
subhead: The first installment of La Bataille de Gaulle, released on June 3, got off to a disappointing start before box office numbers soared, driven by a historic heatwave and word of mouth. This is especially significant, as the film resonates with current events, writes Le Monde's culture columnist.

subhead: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: The Christian right's charge to rewrite American history
subhead: 'America 250' (12/13). Barack Obama's election in 2008 led to greater recognition of the role of minorities in nation-building. But the conservative backlash soon followed. Leading the 'patriotic' counter-offensive, Donald Trump has received support from the influential Hillsdale College.

subhead: Harvard University economics professor and former International Monetary Fund official Gita Gopinath expresses concern over the level of US federal debt.
